LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER WEDBUSH SECURITIES INC.'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - PETITIONER'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE ARBITRATION PANEL DID NOT ABUSE THEIR DISCRETION OR EXCEED THEIR POWERS BY DENYING PETITIONER'S REQUEST FOR POSTPONEMENT. PETITIONERS FAILED TO SHOW GOOD CAUSE TO THE PANEL WHICH WOULD WARRANT A POSTPONEMENT. THE PANEL ALSO PROPERLY EXCLUDED PETITIONER'S EVIDENCE AS TO THE INVESTIGATION INTO RESPONDENTS TRADING ACTIVITIES. PETITIONER FAILED TO DISCLOSE THIS EVIDENCE TO RESPONDENT 20 DAYS PRIOR TO THE FIRST DAY OF ARBITRATION. ACCORDINGLY, THE ARBITRATION PANEL WAS REQUIRED TO EXCLUDE THE EVIDENCE UNDER FINRA RULE 13514 IN ABSENCE OF ANY SHOWING BY PETITIONER THAT THERE WAS GOOD CAUSE FOR THE PANEL TO ADMIT THE EVIDENCE IN LIGHT OF THE RULE VIOLATION. FINALLY, THERE WAS NO BASIS FOR DISQUALIFICATION OF ARBITRATOR HITCHCOCK. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

calendar.matters:
Order To Show Cause As To Petitioner And Cross-Respondent Wedbush Securities Inc. Re: Failure To Deliver Stock Options Per The Court'S 9/7/11 Order
Off Calendar Nov-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JOHN ERIKSON, ESQ,. 213.688.6665, FOR PETITIONER WEDBUSH SECURITIES, INC.
DAVID COMMINS, ESQ., 415.391.6490, FOR RESPONDENT STEPHEN KELLEHER.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling re: Order To Show Cause As To Petitioner And Cross-Respondent Wedbush Securities Inc. Re: Failure To Deliver Stock Options Per The Court'S 9/7/11 Order. The OSC regarding contempt is denied. Mr. Kelleher fails to show beyond a reasonable doubt that Wedbush has acted contemptuously given the uncertainty regarding the terms of the stock options. Order signed in open court. revised stipulation and order granting release of remainder of funds on deposit after appeal signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

MINI MINUTES: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE TO PETITIONER AND CROSS-RESPONDENT WEDBUSH SECURITIES INC. RE: FAILURE TO DELIVER STOCK OPTIONS PER THE COURT'S SEPTEMBER 7, 2011 ORDER.

Appearances: John Erikson of Wedbush Securities, Inc. (213.688.6665) for petitioner Wedbush Securities, Inc.; David H. S. Commins of Commins & Knudsen (415.391.6490) for respondent Stephen Kelleher.

Argued and the Court rules as follows: Continued to November 19, 2013 for further hearing. Counsel may submit supplemental briefs not to exceed 5 pages. Petitioner's brief to be filed and served by October 28, 2013; Respondent's brief to be filed and served by November 4, 2013. Counsel agree to service by email. The Court signs a stipulated order to release the remaining funds on deposit to petitioner. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR #6185 (302/MJM)

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - PETITIONER'S PETITION TO VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE ARBITRATION PANEL DID NOT ABUSE THEIR DISCRETION OR EXCEED THEIR POWERS BY DENYING PETITIONER'S REQUEST FOR POSTPONEMENT. PETITIONERS FAILED TO SHOW GOOD CAUSE TO THE PANEL WHICH WOULD WARRANT A POSTPONEMENT. THE PANEL ALSO PROPERLY EXCLUDED PETITIONER'S EVIDENCE AS TO THE INVESTIGATION INTO RESPONDENTS TRADING ACTIVITIES. PETITIONER FAILED TO DISCLOSE THIS EVIDENCE TO RESPONDENT 20 DAYS PRIOR TO THE FIRST DAY OF ARBITRATION. ACCORDINGLY, THE ARBITRATION PANEL WAS REQUIRED TO EXCLUDE THE EVIDENCE UNDER FINRA RULE 13514 IN ABSENCE OF ANY SHOWING BY PETITIONER THAT THERE WAS GOOD CAUSE FOR THE PANEL TO ADMIT THE EVIDENCE IN LIGHT OF THE RULE VIOLATION.
